flags.description: Systematic apostrophe character encoding defect: All 34 apostrophes in the authoritative PDF use the typographic right single quotation mark (U+2019 ''') but the DB has converted all of them to the ASCII apostrophe (U+0027 '''). This is a consistent, document-wide OCR or text ingestion error affecting possessive forms and contractions throughout the opinion (e.g., 'Graff's', 'State's', 'court's', 'didn't', 'We're', 'wouldn't', 'doesn't', 'don't', 'that's', 'you'd', 'victim's', 'defendant's', 'detective's', 'prosecutor's'). The correct form per the authoritative PDF source is the right single quotation mark character.

proposals.evidence: The DB text shows a truncated/corrupted quote in paragraph [¶7] reading 'the performance inquiry must be whether the circumstances.' followed by orphaned/garbled text ('counsel's assistance was considering all reasonable'). The PDF shows the complete, correct quote: 'the performance inquiry must be whether counsel's assistance was reasonable considering all the circumstances.' This is part of the discussion of Strickland's holding in the Roe v. Flores-Ortega analysis. The error represents missing middle text ('counsel's assistance was reasonable considering all the ') from a broken sentence.

flags.location_quote: unless: 1. 2. 3. 4. 5. 6. The order is not in accordance with the law. The order is in violation of the constitutional rights of the appellant. flags.description: Statutory list items (N.D.C.C. § 28-32-46 criteria) have been consolidated into a single paragraph. The DB text has all list numbers (1-8) presented together at the start ('1. 2. 3. 4. 5. 6. ... 7. 8.') followed by all item texts concatenated, whereas the authoritative PDF properly interleaves each number with its corresponding text and line breaks (e.g., '1. The order is not in accordance with the law.' followed by '2. The order is in violation of the constitutional rights of the appellant.', etc.). All words and content are present and correct; this is a structural/formatting issue from OCR/text extraction that collapsed the list layout.

flags.location_quote: H.L.B. appears 3 times; H.R.B. appears 26 times in database
flags.description: Pattern anomaly: OCR engine consistently confused R with L only in the child's initials (H.R.B.) in three consecutive paragraphs (21-23), while correctly reading H.R.B. in all other locations. This is a classic OCR failure pattern where similar letters are misread in specific contexts.
